import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Mountain, Road, Hybrid
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    available = Column(Boolean, default=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    part_number = Column(String)
    bicycle_compatibility = Column(String) # List of compatible bicycle models, comma separated
    available = Column(Boolean, default=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., Helmet, Lights, Locks
    available = Column(Boolean, default=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_name = Column(String)
    customer_email = Column(String)
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)

    items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer={self.customer_name}, total={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String) # 'bicycle', 'spare_part', 'accessory'
    item_id = Column(Integer) # ID of the bicycle, spare part, or accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type={self.item_type}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def connect(self):
        self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    def add_bicycle(self, name, description, price, brand, model, category, frame_material, wheel_size, available=True):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, model=model, category=category, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, available=available)
        self.session.add(bicycle)
        self.session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

    def update_bicycle(self, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return bicycle
        return None

    def delete_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            self.session.delete(bicycle)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_bicycles(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.description.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.brand.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.model.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_bicycles(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def add_spare_part(self, name, description, price, brand, part_number, bicycle_compatibility, available=True):
        spare_part = SparePart(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, part_number=part_number, bicycle_compatibility=bicycle_compatibility, available=available)
        self.session.add(spare_part)
        self.session.commit()
        return spare_part

    def get_spare_part(self, spare_part_id):
        return self.session.query(SparePart).filter(SparePart.id == spare_part_id).first()

    def update_spare_part(self, spare_part_id, **kwargs):
        spare_part = self.get_spare_part(spare_part_id)
        if spare_part:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(spare_part, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return spare_part
        return None

    def delete_spare_part(self, spare_part_id):
        spare_part = self.get_spare_part(spare_part_id)
        if spare_part:
            self.session.delete(spare_part)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_spare_parts(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(SparePart).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                SparePart.name.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.description.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.brand.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.part_number.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_spare_parts(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(SparePart)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(SparePart, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(SparePart, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def add_accessory(self, name, description, price, brand, category, available=True):
        accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, category=category, available=available)
        self.session.add(accessory)
        self.session.commit()
        return accessory

    def get_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(Accessory.id == accessory_id).first()

    def update_accessory(self, accessory_id, **kwargs):
        accessory = self.get_accessory(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(accessory, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return accessory
        return None

    def delete_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        accessory = self.get_accessory(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            self.session.delete(accessory)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_accessories(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Accessory.name.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.description.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.brand.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.category.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_accessories(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(Accessory)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Accessory, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Accessory, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def create_order(self, customer_name, customer_email, items):
        """
        Creates a new order.

        Args:
            customer_name (str): The name of the customer.
            customer_email (str): The email of the customer.
            items (list): A list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents an item in the order.
                           Each dictionary should have the following keys:
                               - item_type (str): 'bicycle', 'spare_part', or 'accessory'
                               - item_id (int): The ID of the item.
                               - quantity (int): The quantity of the item.

        Returns:
            Order: The created order object.
        """

        total_amount = 0
        order_items = []

        for item_data in items:
            item_type = item_data['item_type']
            item_id = item_data['item_id']
            quantity = item_data['quantity']

            if item_type == 'bicycle':
                item = self.get_bicycle(item_id)
            elif item_type == 'spare_part':
                item = self.get_spare_part(item_id)
            elif item_type == 'accessory':
                item = self.get_accessory(item_id)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid item type: {item_type}")

            if not item:
                raise ValueError(f"Item not found: {item_type} with ID {item_id}")

            total_amount += item.price * quantity
            order_item = OrderItem(item_type=item_type, item_id=item_id, quantity=quantity, price=item.price)
            order_items.append(order_item)

        order = Order(customer_name=customer_name, customer_email=customer_email, total_amount=total_amount, items=order_items)
        self.session.add(order)
        self.session.commit()
        return order

    def get_order(self, order_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter(Order.id == order_id).first()

    accessory1 = db_manager.add_accessory(name="Helmet", description="Protective helmet", price=50.00, brand="Giro", category="Helmet")

    # Create an order
    items = [
        {'item_type': 'bicycle', 'item_id': bicycle1.id, 'quantity': 1},

    db_manager.delete_bicycle(bicycle2.id)

    db_manager.close()

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def connect(self):
        self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    def add_bicycle(self, name, description, price, brand, model, category, frame_material, wheel_size, available=True):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, model=model, category=category, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, available=available)
        self.session.add(bicycle)
        self.session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

    def update_bicycle(self, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return bicycle
        return None

    def delete_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            self.session.delete(bicycle)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_bicycles(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.description.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.brand.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.model.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_bicycles(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def add_spare_part(self, name, description, price, brand, part_number, bicycle_compatibility, available=True):
        spare_part = SparePart(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, part_number=part_number, bicycle_compatibility=bicycle_compatibility, available=available)
        self.session.add(spare_part)
        self.session.commit()
        return spare_part

    def get_spare_part(self, spare_part_id):
        return self.session.query(SparePart).filter(SparePart.id == spare_part_id).first()

    def update_spare_part(self, spare_part_id, **kwargs):
        spare_part = self.get_spare_part(spare_part_id)
        if spare_part:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(spare_part, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return spare_part
        return None

    def delete_spare_part(self, spare_part_id):
        spare_part = self.get_spare_part(spare_part_id)
        if spare_part:
            self.session.delete(spare_part)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_spare_parts(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(SparePart).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                SparePart.name.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.description.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.brand.contains(search_term),
                SparePart.part_number.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_spare_parts(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(SparePart)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(SparePart, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(SparePart, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def add_accessory(self, name, description, price, brand, category, available=True):
        accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, category=category, available=available)
        self.session.add(accessory)
        self.session.commit()
        return accessory

    def get_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(Accessory.id == accessory_id).first()

    def update_accessory(self, accessory_id, **kwargs):
        accessory = self.get_accessory(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(accessory, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return accessory
        return None

    def delete_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        accessory = self.get_accessory(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            self.session.delete(accessory)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_accessories(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Accessory.name.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.description.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.brand.contains(search_term),
                Accessory.category.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_accessories(self, **filters):
        query = self.session.query(Accessory)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Accessory, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Accessory, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def create_order(self, customer_name, customer_email, items):
        """
        Creates a new order.

        Args:
            customer_name (str): The name of the customer.
            customer_email (str): The email of the customer.
            items (list): A list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents an item in the order.
                           Each dictionary should have the following keys:
                               - item_type (str): 'bicycle', 'spare_part', or 'accessory'
                               - item_id (int): The ID of the item.
                               - quantity (int): The quantity of the item.

        Returns:
            Order: The created order object.
        """

        total_amount = 0
        order_items = []

        for item_data in items:
            item_type = item_data['item_type']
            item_id = item_data['item_id']
            quantity = item_data['quantity']

            if item_type == 'bicycle':
                item = self.get_bicycle(item_id)
            elif item_type == 'spare_part':
                item = self.get_spare_part(item_id)
            elif item_type == 'accessory':
                item = self.get_accessory(item_id)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid item type: {item_type}")

            if not item:
                raise ValueError(f"Item not found: {item_type} with ID {item_id}")

            total_amount += item.price * quantity
            order_item = OrderItem(item_type=item_type, item_id=item_id, quantity=quantity, price=item.price)
            order_items.append(order_item)

        order = Order(customer_name=customer_name, customer_email=customer_email, total_amount=total_amount, items=order_items)
        self.session.add(order)
        self.session.commit()
        return order

    def get_order(self, order_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter(Order.id == order_id).first()

if __name__ == '__main__':

    spare_part2 = db_manager.add_spare_part(name="Chain", description="Durable chain", price=30.00, brand="SRAM", part_number="PC-1110", bicycle_compatibility="X-Caliber 7")

    # Add some accessories
    accessory1 = db_manager.add_accessory(name="Helmet", description="Protective helmet", price=50.00, brand="Giro", category="Helmet")

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    description = Column(String)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"

    parts = relationship("Part", secondary="bicycle_parts", back_populates="bicycles")
    accessories = relationship("Accessory", secondary="bicycle_accessories", back_populates="bicycles")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"


class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Brakes", "Gears", "Wheels"
    manufacturer = Column(String)

    bicycles = relationship("Bicycle", secondary="bicycle_parts", back_populates="parts")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', manufacturer='{self.manufacturer}')>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    brand = Column(String)

    bicycles = relationship("Bicycle", secondary="bicycle_accessories", back_populates="accessories")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', brand='{self.brand}')>"


class BicyclePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_parts'
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), primary_key=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), primary_key=True)


class BicycleAccessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_accessories'
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), primary_key=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), primary_key=True)


class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default="Pending") # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered", "Cancelled"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount}, status='{self.status}')>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String) # "Bicycle", "Part", "Accessory"
    item_id = Column(Integer) # ID of the Bicycle, Part, or Accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


# Database connection
DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./bicycle_shop.db"  # Use SQLite for simplicity
engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL)
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Session creation
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


# Example CRUD operations (using a dependency injection pattern for the database session)
def add_bicycle(db: SessionLocal, name: str, brand: str, model: str, price: float, description: str, stock_quantity: int, category: str):
    db_bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, price=price, description=description, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category=category)
    db.add(db_bicycle)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_bicycle)
    return db_bicycle

def get_bicycle(db: SessionLocal, bicycle_id: int):
    return db.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

def update_bicycle(db: SessionLocal, bicycle_id: int, name: str = None, brand: str = None, model: str = None, price: float = None, description: str = None, stock_quantity: int = None, category: str = None):
    db_bicycle = db.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()
    if db_bicycle:
        if name is not None:
            db_bicycle.name = name
        if brand is not None:
            db_bicycle.brand = brand
        if model is not None:
            db_bicycle.model = model
        if price is not None:
            db_bicycle.price = price
        if description is not None:
            db_bicycle.description = description
        if stock_quantity is not None:
            db_bicycle.stock_quantity = stock_quantity
        if category is not None:
            db_bicycle.category = category
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_bicycle)
        return db_bicycle
    return None

def delete_bicycle(db: SessionLocal, bicycle_id: int):
    db_bicycle = db.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()
    if db_bicycle:
        db.delete(db_bicycle)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def search_bicycles(db: SessionLocal, query: str):
    return db.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.name.contains(query) | Bicycle.brand.contains(query) | Bicycle.model.contains(query) | Bicycle.description.contains(query)).all()

def filter_bicycles(db: SessionLocal, category: str = None, min_price: float = None, max_price: float = None):
    query = db.query(Bicycle)
    if category:
        query = query.filter(Bicycle.category == category)
    if min_price:
        query = query.filter(Bicycle.price >= min_price)
    if max_price:
        query = query.filter(Bicycle.price <= max_price)
    return query.all()

# Example Usage (demonstrates opening/closing connection, CRUD, search, and filter)
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Get a database session
    db_generator = get_db()
    db = next(db_generator) # Get the session object

    # Add a bicycle
    new_bicycle = add_bicycle(db, name="Mountain Bike Pro", brand="Awesome Bikes", model="X1000", price=799.99, description="A great mountain bike for all terrains.", stock_quantity=10, category="Mountain")
    print(f"Added bicycle: {new_bicycle}")

    # Get the bicycle
    retrieved_bicycle = get_bicycle(db, new_bicycle.id)
    print(f"Retrieved bicycle: {retrieved_bicycle}")

    # Update the bicycle
    updated_bicycle = update_bicycle(db, new_bicycle.id, price=849.99, stock_quantity=5)
    print(f"Updated bicycle: {updated_bicycle}")

    # Search for bicycles
    search_results = search_bicycles(db, "Awesome")
    print(f"Search results: {search_results}")

    # Filter bicycles
    filtered_bicycles = filter_bicycles(db, category="Mountain", min_price=700)
    print(f"Filtered bicycles: {filtered_bicycles}")

    # Add a part
    new_part = add_part(db, name="Shimano Brakes", description="High quality brakes", price=50.00, stock_quantity=20, category="Brakes", manufacturer="Shimano")
    print(f"Added part: {new_part}")

    # Add an accessory
    new_accessory = add_accessory(db, name="Bike Helmet", description="Protective helmet", price=30.00, stock_quantity=30, category="Helmets", brand="SafetyGear")
    print(f"Added accessory: {new_accessory}")

    # Associate the part and accessory with the bicycle
    retrieved_bicycle.parts.append(new_part)
    retrieved_bicycle.accessories.append(new_accessory)
    db.commit()

    # Verify the association
    retrieved_bicycle = get_bicycle(db, new_bicycle.id)
    print(f"Bicycle parts: {retrieved_bicycle.parts}")
    print(f"Bicycle accessories: {retrieved_bicycle.accessories}")

    # Delete the bicycle (optional - be careful!)
    # delete_bicycle(db, new_bicycle.id)
    # print("Bicycle deleted.")

    # Close the database session (implicitly done by the 'finally' block in get_db)
    try:
        db_generator.close()
    except AttributeError:
        pass # Already closed

def add_part(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str, price: float, stock_quantity: int, category: str, manufacturer: str):
    db_part = Part(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category=category, manufacturer=manufacturer)
    db.add(db_part)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_part)
    return db_part

def add_accessory(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str, price: float, stock_quantity: int, category: str, brand: str):
    db_accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category=category, brand=brand)
    db.add(db_accessory)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_accessory)
    return db_accessory

import sqlalchemy

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    description = Column(String)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"

    parts = relationship("Part", secondary="bicycle_parts", back_populates="bicycles")
    accessories = relationship("Accessory", secondary="bicycle_accessories", back_populates="bicycles")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"


class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Brakes", "Gears", "Wheels"
    manufacturer = Column(String)

    bicycles = relationship("Bicycle", secondary="bicycle_parts", back_populates="parts")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', manufacturer='{self.manufacturer}')>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    brand = Column(String)

    bicycles = relationship("Bicycle", secondary="bicycle_accessories", back_populates="accessories")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', brand='{self.brand}')>"


class BicyclePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_parts'
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), primary_key=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), primary_key=True)


class BicycleAccessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_accessories'
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), primary_key=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), primary_key=True)


class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default="Pending") # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered", "Cancelled"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount}, status='{self.status}')>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String) # "Bicycle", "Part", "Accessory"
    item_id = Column(Integer) # ID of the Bicycle, Part, or Accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    description = Column(String)
    quantity = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="parts")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

Bicycle.parts = relationship("Part", order_by=Part.id, back_populates="bicycle")

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="accessories")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

Bicycle.accessories = relationship("Accessory", order_by=Accessory.id, back_populates="bicycle")


class BicycleShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def open_session(self):
        self.session = self.Session()

    def close_session(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    def add_bicycle(self, name, model, price, description, quantity):
        self.open_session()
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, model=model, price=price, description=description, quantity=quantity)
        self.session.add(bicycle)
        self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        self.open_session()
        bicycle = self.session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=bicycle_id).first()
        self.close_session()
        return bicycle

    def update_bicycle(self, bicycle_id, name=None, model=None, price=None, description=None, quantity=None):
        self.open_session()
        bicycle = self.session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=bicycle_id).first()
        if bicycle:
            if name is not None:
                bicycle.name = name
            if model is not None:
                bicycle.model = model
            if price is not None:
                bicycle.price = price
            if description is not None:
                bicycle.description = description
            if quantity is not None:
                bicycle.quantity = quantity
            self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()
        return bicycle

    def delete_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        self.open_session()
        bicycle = self.session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=bicycle_id).first()
        if bicycle:
            self.session.delete(bicycle)
            self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()

    def search_bicycles(self, search_term):
        self.open_session()
        bicycles = self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.model.contains(search_term),
                Bicycle.description.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()
        self.close_session()
        return bicycles

    def add_part(self, name, description, price, quantity, bicycle_id):
        self.open_session()
        part = Part(name=name, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, bicycle_id=bicycle_id)
        self.session.add(part)
        self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()
        return part

    def get_part(self, part_id):
        self.open_session()
        part = self.session.query(Part).filter_by(id=part_id).first()
        self.close_session()
        return part

    def update_part(self, part_id, name=None, description=None, price=None, quantity=None, bicycle_id=None):
        self.open_session()
        part = self.session.query(Part).filter_by(id=part_id).first()
        if part:
            if name is not None:
                part.name = name
            if description is not None:
                part.description = description
            if price is not None:
                part.price = price
            if quantity is not None:
                part.quantity = quantity
            if bicycle_id is not None:
                part.bicycle_id = bicycle_id
            self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()
        return part

    def delete_part(self, part_id):
        self.open_session()
        part = self.session.query(Part).filter_by(id=part_id).first()
        if part:
            self.session.delete(part)
            self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()

    def search_parts(self, search_term):
        self.open_session()
        parts = self.session.query(Part).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Part.name.contains(search_term),
                Part.description.contains(search_term)
            )
        ).all()
        self.close_session()
        return parts

    def add_accessory(self, name, description, price, quantity, bicycle_id):
        self.open_session()
        accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, bicycle_id=bicycle_id)
        self.session.add(accessory)
        self.session.commit()
        self.close_session()
        return accessory

    def get_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        self.open_session()
        accessory = self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()
        self.close_session()
        return accessory

    def update_accessory(self, accessory_id, name=None, description=None, price=None, quantity=None, bicycle_id=None):
        self.open_session()
        accessory = self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()
        if accessory:
            if name is not None:
                accessory.name = name
            if description is not None:
                accessory.description = description
            if price is not None:

                accessory.quantity = quantity

            self.session.commit()

    def delete_accessory(self, accessory_id):

        accessory = self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    description = Column(String)
    quantity = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", backref="spare_parts")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", backref="accessories")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, quantity={self.quantity})>"


class BicycleShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_url='sqlite:///:memory:'):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def open_connection(self):
        self.session = self.Session()

    def close_connection(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

import sqlite3

class BicycleShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_name="bicycle_shop.db"):
        self.db_name = db_name
        self.conn = None
        self.cursor = None

    def open_connection(self):
        """Opens a connection to the SQLite database."""
        try:
            self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_name)
            self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
            print(f"Successfully connected to database: {self.db_name}")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error connecting to database: {e}")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def close_connection(self):
        """Closes the connection to the SQLite database."""
        if self.conn:
            self.conn.close()
            print("Connection closed.")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def create_tables(self):
        """Creates the necessary tables in the database."""
        if not self.conn or not self.cursor:
            print("Database connection not established. Call open_connection() first.")
            return

        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bicycles (
                    bicycle_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    brand TEXT,
                    model TEXT,
                    type TEXT,
                    frame_material TEXT,
                    wheel_size INTEGER,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS parts (
                    part_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    category TEXT,
                    brand TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS accessories (
                    accessory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    type TEXT,
                    brand TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.conn.commit()
            print("Tables created successfully.")

        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error creating tables: {e}")

    def add_bicycle(self, name, brand, model, type, frame_material, wheel_size, price, quantity=0):
        """Adds a new bicycle to the bicycles table."""
        if not self.conn or not self.cursor:
            print("Database connection not established. Call open_connection() first.")
            return

        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO bicycles (name, brand, model, type, frame_material, wheel_size, price, quantity)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """, (name, brand, model, type, frame_material, wheel_size, price, quantity))
            self.conn.commit()
            print(f"Bicycle '{name}' added successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error adding bicycle: {e}")

            return

        try:

                INSERT INTO parts (name, description, category, brand, price, quantity)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)

            self.conn.commit()
            print(f"Part '{name}' added successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:

    def get_bicycles(self, filters=None, search_term=None):

import sqlite3

        try:
            self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_name)
            self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
            print(f"Successfully connected to database: {self.db_name}")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error connecting to database: {e}")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def close_connection(self):
        """Closes the connection to the SQLite database."""
        if self.conn:
            self.conn.close()
            print("Connection closed.")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def create_tables(self):
        """Creates the necessary tables in the database."""
        if not self.conn or not self.cursor:
            print("Database connection not established. Call open_connection() first.")
            return

        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bicycles (
                    bicycle_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    brand TEXT,
                    model TEXT,
                    type TEXT,
                    frame_material TEXT,
                    wheel_size INTEGER,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0

            """)

    def get_bicycles(self, filters=None, search_term=None):
        """Retrieves bicycles from the bicycles table based on filters and search term."""
        if not self.conn or not self.cursor:
            print("Database connection not established. Call open_connection() first.")
            return []

        try:
            sql = "SELECT * FROM bicycles"
            where_clauses = []
            values = []

            if filters:
                for key, value in filters.items():
                    where_clauses.append(f"{key} = ?")
                    values.append(value)

            if search_term:
                where_clauses.append("name LIKE ? OR brand LIKE ? OR model LIKE ? OR type LIKE ? OR frame_material LIKE ?")
                search_pattern = f"%{search_term}%"
                values.extend([search_pattern] * 5)

            if where_clauses:
                sql += " WHERE " + " AND ".join(where_clauses)

            self.cursor.execute(sql, tuple(values))
            rows = self.cursor.fetchall()
            return rows

import sqlite3

class BicycleShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_name="bicycle_shop.db"):
        self.db_name = db_name
        self.conn = None
        self.cursor = None

    def open_connection(self):
        """Opens a connection to the SQLite database."""
        try:
            self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_name)
            self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
            print(f"Successfully connected to database: {self.db_name}")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error connecting to database: {e}")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def close_connection(self):
        """Closes the connection to the SQLite database."""
        if self.conn:
            self.conn.close()
            print("Connection closed.")
            self.conn = None
            self.cursor = None

    def create_tables(self):
        """Creates the necessary tables in the database."""
        if not self.conn or not self.cursor:
            print("Database connection not established. Call open_connection() first.")
            return

        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bicycles (
                    bicycle_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    brand TEXT,
                    model TEXT,
                    type TEXT,
                    frame_material TEXT,
                    wheel_size INTEGER,
                    gears INTEGER,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS parts (
                    part_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    category TEXT,
                    brand TEXT,
                    model TEXT,
                    bicycle_id INTEGER,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    FOREIGN KEY (bicycle_id) REFERENCES bicycles(bicycle_id)
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS accessories (
                    accessory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    category TEXT,
                    brand TEXT,
                    model TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT
                )
            """)

            self.conn.commit()
            print("Tables created successfully.")

        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error creating tables: {e}")

        """Adds a new bicycle to the bicycles table."""

            return

        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO bicycles (name, brand, model, type, frame_material, wheel_size, gears, price, description)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """, (name, brand, model, type, frame_material, wheel_size, gears, price, description))
            self.conn.commit()
            print("Bicycle added successfully.")

                update_values.append(description)
            if price is not None:
                update_fields.append("price = ?")
                update_values.append(price)
            if quantity is not None:

            self.conn.commit()
            print("Accessory updated successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error updating accessory: {e}")
            self.conn.rollback()
            raise

    def get_accessory(self, accessory_id):
        """Retrieves an accessory from the database by ID."""
        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                SELECT * FROM accessories WHERE accessory_id = ?
            """, (accessory_id,))
            accessory = self.cursor.fetchone()
            return accessory
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error retrieving accessory: {e}")
            return None

    def search_accessories(self, search_term):
        """Searches for accessories in the database based on a search term."""
        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                SELECT * FROM accessories
                WHERE name LIKE ? OR description LIKE ?
            """, (f"%{search_term}%", f"%{search_term}%"))
            accessories = self.cursor.fetchall()
            return accessories
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error searching accessories: {e}")
            return None

import sqlite3

class BicycleShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_name="bicycle_shop.db"):
        self.db_name = db_name
        self.conn = None
        self.cursor = None

    def connect(self):
        """Connects to the SQLite database."""
        try:
            self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_name)
            self.cursor = self.conn.cursor()
            print("Successfully connected to the database.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error connecting to the database: {e}")
            raise

    def close(self):
        """Closes the database connection."""
        if self.conn:
            self.conn.close()
            print("Database connection closed.")

    def create_tables(self):
        """Creates the necessary tables in the database."""
        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Bicycles (
                    bicycle_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    model TEXT NOT NULL,
                    brand TEXT NOT NULL,
                    type TEXT,
                    size INTEGER,
                    color TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS SpareParts (
                    part_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    bicycle_type TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.cursor.execute("""
                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Accessories (
                    accessory_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
                    description TEXT,
                    price REAL NOT NULL,
                    quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0
                )
            """)

            self.conn.commit()
            print("Tables created successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error creating tables: {e}")
            raise

    def add_bicycle(self, model, brand, type, size, color, price, quantity=0):
        """Adds a new bicycle to the database."""
        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO Bicycles (model, brand, type, size, color, price, quantity)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """, (model, brand, type, size, color, price, quantity))
            self.conn.commit()
            print(f"Bicycle '{model}' added successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error adding bicycle: {e}")
            raise

    def delete_bicycle(self, bicycle_id):
        """Deletes a bicycle from the database."""
        try:
            self.cursor.execute("""
                DELETE FROM Bicycles WHERE bicycle_id = ?
            """, (bicycle_id,))
            self.conn.commit()
            print(f"Bicycle with ID {bicycle_id} deleted successfully.")
        except sqlite3.Error as e:
            print(f"Error deleting bicycle: {e}")
            raise

    def update_bicycle(self, bicycle_id, model=None, brand=None, type=None, size=None, color=None, price=None, quantity=None):
        """Updates bicycle information in the database."""
        try:
            update_fields = []
            update_values = []

            if model is not None:
                update_fields.append("model = ?")
                update_values.append(model)
            if brand is not None:
                update_fields.append("brand = ?")
                update_values.append(brand)
            if type is not None:
                update_fields.append("type = ?")
                update_values.append(type)
            if size is not None:
                update_fields.append("size = ?")
                update_values.append(size)
            if color is not None:

                update_values.append(color)
            if price is not None:

                update_values.append(price)
            if quantity is not None:

                update_values.append(quantity)

            if not update_fields:
                print("No fields to update.")
                return

            sql = f"""
                UPDATE Bicycles
                SET {', '.join(update_fields)}
                WHERE bicycle_id = ?
            """
            update_values.append(bicycle_id)

            self.cursor.execute(sql, tuple(update_values))
            self.conn.commit()

        """Retrieves a bicycle from the database by ID."""
        try:

            """, (bicycle_id,))
            bicycle = self.cursor.fetchone()
            return bicycle
        except sqlite3.Error as e:

            raise

    def search_bicycles(self, search_term):

        try:

            """, (f"%{search_term}%", f"%{search_term}%", f"%{search_term}%", f"%{search_term}%"))

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    model_year = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_compatibility = Column(String)  # List of bicycle models it fits
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Consider using DateTime
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String) # "bicycle", "spare_part", "accessory"
    item_id = Column(Integer) # ID of the bicycle, spare part, or accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', quantity={self.quantity})>"

# Database connection
engine = create_engine('sqlite:///:memory:')  # In-memory SQLite database for demonstration
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

# Session creation
Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
session = Session()

# Example data insertion
bicycle1 = Bicycle(name="Mountain Bike Pro", description="High-performance mountain bike", price=899.99, brand="Trek", model_year=2023, category="Mountain", in_stock=10)
spare_part1 = SparePart(name="Brake Pads", description="Set of brake pads", price=19.99, bicycle_compatibility="Mountain Bike Pro", in_stock=50)
accessory1 = Accessory(name="Helmet", description="Safety helmet", price=49.99, category="Helmets", in_stock=20)
customer1 = Customer(first_name="John", last_name="Doe", email="john.doe@example.com", phone_number="555-1234", address="123 Main St")

session.add_all([bicycle1, spare_part1, accessory1, customer1])
session.commit()

# Example queries

# Selecting all bicycles
bicycles = session.query(Bicycle).all()
print("All Bicycles:", bicycles)

# Filtering by brand
trek_bikes = session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.brand == "Trek").all()
print("Trek Bikes:", trek_bikes)

# Searching for items containing "brake" in the name (case-insensitive)
brake_parts = session.query(SparePart).filter(SparePart.name.ilike("%brake%")).all()
print("Spare Parts with 'brake' in name:", brake_parts)

# Updating the price of a bicycle
bicycle1.price = 949.99
session.commit()
print("Updated Bicycle Price:", bicycle1)

# Deleting a spare part
session.delete(spare_part1)
session.commit()

#Adding an order
order1 = Order(customer_id=customer1.id, order_date="2023-11-15", total_amount=bicycle1.price, status="Pending")
session.add(order1)
session.commit()

order_item1 = OrderItem(order_id=order1.id, item_type="bicycle", item_id=bicycle1.id, quantity=1, price=bicycle1.price)
session.add(order_item1)
session.commit()

#Filtered search on orders
pending_orders = session.query(Order).filter(Order.status == "Pending").all()
print("Pending Orders:", pending_orders)

#Joining tables (Customer and Orders)
customer_orders = session.query(Customer, Order).join(Order).filter(Customer.first_name == "John").all()
print("John Doe's Orders:", customer_orders)

#Closing the session
session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    model_year = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    bicycle_compatibility = Column(String)  # List of bicycle models it fits
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    category = Column(String) # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Consider using DateTime
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String) # "bicycle", "spare_part", "accessory"
    item_id = Column(Integer) # ID of the bicycle, spare part, or accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', quantity={self.quantity})>"

import sqlite3

def create_database(db_name="zcheHz"):
    """
    Creates a SQLite database with tables for businesses, wards, and activities.
    """
    try:
        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_name + ".db")
        cursor = conn.cursor()

        # Create Businesses table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Businesses (
                business_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                business_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                business_type TEXT,
                address TEXT,
                ward_id INTEGER,
                FOREIGN KEY (ward_id) REFERENCES Wards(ward_id)
            )
        """)

        # Create Wards table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Wards (
                ward_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                ward_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                population INTEGER
            )
        """)

        # Create Activities table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Activities (
                activity_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                activity_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                description TEXT,
                business_id INTEGER,
                FOREIGN KEY (business_id) REFERENCES Businesses(business_id)
            )
        """)

        conn.commit()
        print(f"Database '{db_name}' created successfully.")
        return conn, cursor

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error creating database: {e}")
        if conn:
            conn.rollback()
        return None, None

def insert_data(conn, cursor, table_name, data):
    """
    Inserts data into a specified table.
    """
    try:
        if table_name == "Businesses":
            cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO Businesses (business_name, business_type, address, ward_id)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
            """, (data['business_name'], data['business_type'], data['address'], data['ward_id']))
        elif table_name == "Wards":
            cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO Wards (ward_name, population)
                VALUES (?, ?)
            """, (data['ward_name'], data['population']))
        elif table_name == "Activities":
            cursor.execute("""
                INSERT INTO Activities (activity_name, description, business_id)
                VALUES (?, ?, ?)
            """, (data['activity_name'], data['description'], data['business_id']))
        else:
            print(f"Invalid table name: {table_name}")
            return False

        conn.commit()
        print(f"Data inserted into '{table_name}' successfully.")
        return True

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error inserting data into '{table_name}': {e}")
        conn.rollback()
        return False

def query_data(conn, cursor, table_name, condition=None):
    """
    Queries data from a specified table based on an optional condition.
    """
    try:
        if condition:
            query = f"SELECT * FROM {table_name} WHERE {condition}"
        else:
            query = f"SELECT * FROM {table_name}"

        cursor.execute(query)
        rows = cursor.fetchall()

        if rows:
            print(f"Query results from '{table_name}':")
            for row in rows:
                print(row)
            return rows
        else:
            print(f"No data found in '{table_name}' matching the condition.")
            return []

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error querying data from '{table_name}': {e}")
        return []

def update_data(conn, cursor, table_name, set_values, condition):
    """
    Updates data in a specified table based on a condition.
    """
    try:
        query = f"UPDATE {table_name} SET {set_values} WHERE {condition}"
        cursor.execute(query)
        conn.commit()
        print(f"Data updated in '{table_name}' successfully.")
        return True

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error updating data in '{table_name}': {e}")
        conn.rollback()
        return False

def delete_data(conn, cursor, table_name, condition):
    """
    Deletes data from a specified table based on a condition.
    """
    try:
        query = f"DELETE FROM {table_name} WHERE {condition}"
        cursor.execute(query)
        conn.commit()
        print(f"Data deleted from '{table_name}' successfully.")
        return True

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error deleting data from '{table_name}': {e}")
        conn.rollback()
        return False

def search_data(conn, cursor, table_name, search_term, columns):
    """
    Searches for data in a specified table based on a search term across multiple columns.
    """
    try:
        conditions = [f"{col} LIKE ?" for col in columns]
        where_clause = " OR ".join(conditions)
        query = f"SELECT * FROM {table_name} WHERE {where_clause}"
        search_pattern = f"%{search_term}%"
        cursor.execute(query, [search_pattern] * len(columns))
        rows = cursor.fetchall()

        if rows:
            print(f"Search results from '{table_name}':")
            for row in rows:
                print(row)
            return rows
        else:
            print(f"No data found in '{table_name}' matching the search term.")
            return []

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error searching data in '{table_name}': {e}")
        return []

def close_connection(conn):
    """
    Closes the database connection.
    """
    if conn:
        conn.close()
        print("Database connection closed.")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_name = "zcheHz"
    conn, cursor = create_database(db_name)

    if conn and cursor:
        # Insert data
        ward_data = {'ward_name': 'Downtown', 'population': 50000}
        insert_data(conn, cursor, "Wards", ward_data)

        business_data = {'business_name': 'Cool Cafe', 'business_type': 'Restaurant', 'address': '123 Main St', 'ward_id': 1}
        insert_data(conn, cursor, "Businesses", business_data)

        activity_data = {'activity_name': 'Live Music', 'description': 'Every Friday night', 'business_id': 1}
        insert_data(conn, cursor, "Activities", activity_data)

        # Query data
        query_data(conn, cursor, "Businesses")
        query_data(conn, cursor, "Wards", "population > 40000")

        # Update data
        update_data(conn, cursor, "Businesses", "business_type = 'Cafe'", "business_name = 'Cool Cafe'")

        # Delete data
        delete_data(conn, cursor, "Activities", "activity_name = 'Live Music'")

        # Search data
        search_data(conn, cursor, "Businesses", "Cafe", ["business_name", "business_type"])

        # Close connection
        close_connection(conn)

import sqlite3

def create_database(db_name="zcheHz"):
    """
    Creates a SQLite database with tables for businesses, wards, and activities.
    """
    try:
        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_name + ".db")
        cursor = conn.cursor()

        # Create Businesses table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Businesses (
                business_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                business_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                business_type TEXT,
                address TEXT,
                ward_id INTEGER,
                FOREIGN KEY (ward_id) REFERENCES Wards(ward_id)
            )
        """)

        # Create Wards table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Wards (
                ward_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                ward_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                population INTEGER
            )
        """)

        # Create Activities table
        cursor.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Activities (
                activity_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                activity_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                activity_description TEXT,
                business_id INTEGER,
                FOREIGN KEY (business_id) REFERENCES Businesses(business_id)
            )
        """)

    except sqlite3.Error as e:

            conn.rollback()
        return None

    finally:
        if conn:
            conn.close()

def perform_operations(db_name="zcheHz"):
    """
    Performs CRUD operations on the database.
    """
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_name + ".db")
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    try:
        # Insert data into Wards
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Wards (ward_name, population) VALUES (?, ?)", ("Downtown", 15000))
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Wards (ward_name, population) VALUES (?, ?)", ("Uptown", 20000))
        conn.commit()

        # Insert data into Businesses
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Businesses (business_name, business_type, address, ward_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                       ("Coffee Shop", "Cafe", "123 Main St", 1))
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Businesses (business_name, business_type, address, ward_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
                       ("Grocery Store", "Retail", "456 Oak Ave", 2))
        conn.commit()

        # Insert data into Activities
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Activities (activity_name, activity_description, business_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                       ("Live Music", "Every Friday night", 1))
        cursor.execute("INSERT INTO Activities (activity_name, activity_description, business_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                       ("Weekly Specials", "Discounts on produce", 2))
        conn.commit()

        # Query data
        cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM Businesses")
        businesses = cursor.fetchall()
        print("\nAll Businesses:")
        for business in businesses:
            print(business)

        # Update data
        cursor.execute("UPDATE Businesses SET business_type = ? WHERE business_name = ?", ("Restaurant", "Coffee Shop"))
        conn.commit()

        # Delete data
        cursor.execute("DELETE FROM Activities WHERE activity_name = ?", ("Weekly Specials",))
        conn.commit()

        # Filter data
        cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM Businesses WHERE ward_id = ?", (1,))
        businesses_in_ward1 = cursor.fetchall()
        print("\nBusinesses in Ward 1:")
        for business in businesses_in_ward1:
            print(business)

        # Search data (example: search for businesses with 'Shop' in their name)
        cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM Businesses WHERE business_name LIKE ?", ('%Shop%',))
        search_results = cursor.fetchall()
        print("\nSearch results for businesses containing 'Shop':")
        for result in search_results:
            print(result)

    except sqlite3.Error as e:
        print(f"Error performing operations: {e}")
        if conn:
            conn.rollback()

    finally:
        if conn:
            conn.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    db_connection = create_database()
    if db_connection:
        perform_operations()
